categories.name: جُمُوعُ تَكْسِيرٍ عَلَى وَزْنِ أَفْعَال
categories.nameEn: Broken plurals on the morphological pattern `أَفْعَال` (af'āl)

items.verse: لَا يَسْتَوِىٓ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۚ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ
items.verseEn: Not equal are the companions of the Fire and the companions of Paradise. The companions of Paradise - they are the attainers [of success].

items.verse: إِنَّمَآ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَأَوْلَـٰدُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌۭ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عِندَهُۥٓ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: Your wealth and your children are but a trial, and Allāh has with Him a great reward.

items.verse: وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ خَلْقُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَـٰفُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَأَلْوَٰنِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍۢ لِّلْعَـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your languages and your colors. Indeed in that are signs for those of knowledge.
Of the living and the dead?

items.verse: ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى يُرْسِلُ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًۭا فَيَبْسُطُهُۥ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ كَيْفَ يَشَآءُ وَيَجْعَلُهُۥ كِسَفًۭا فَتَرَى ٱلْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَـٰلِهِۦ ۖ فَإِذَآ أَصَابَ بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦٓ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ
items.verseEn: It is Allāh who sends the winds, and they stir the clouds and spread them in the sky however He wills, and He makes them fragments so you see the rain emerge from within them. And when He causes it to fall upon whom He wills of His servants, immediately they rejoice

items.verse: وَيَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِٱلْغَمَـٰمِ وَنُزِّلَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ تَنزِيلًا
items.verseEn: And [mention] the Day when the heaven will split open with [emerging] clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent.

items.verse: وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ كِسْفًۭا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ سَاقِطًۭا يَقُولُوا۟ سَحَابٌۭ مَّرْكُومٌۭ
items.verseEn: And if they were to see a fragment from the sky falling, they would say, "[It is merely] clouds heaped up."
By the sky which sends back

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ لِأَنْوَاعٍ مِنَ التَّجَمُّعَاتِ الْبَشَرِيَّةِ
categories.nameEn: Words for different types of social groups or gatherings

items.verse: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ مَا نَفْقَهُ كَثِيرًۭا مِّمَّا تَقُولُ وَإِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِينَا ضَعِيفًۭا ۖ وَلَوْلَا رَهْطُكَ لَرَجَمْنَـٰكَ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْنَا بِعَزِيزٍۢ
items.verseEn: They said, "O Shuʿayb, we do not understand much of what you say, and indeed, we consider you among us as weak. And if not for your family, we would have stoned you [to death]; and you are not to us one respected."

items.verse: قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسَـٰحِرٌ عَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: Said the eminent among the people of Pharaoh, "Indeed, this is a learned magician
Then let him call his associates;
A [large] company of the former peoples

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَدُلُّ عَلَى حَالَاتِ الْهَلَاكِ أَوِ الْخَلَلِ أَوِ الْإِعْيَاءِ
categories.nameEn: Words denoting states of ruin, flaw, or exhaustion

items.verse: هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَخْرَجَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِمْ لِأَوَّلِ ٱلْحَشْرِ ۚ مَا ظَنَنتُمْ أَن يَخْرُجُوا۟ ۖ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَنَّهُم مَّانِعَتُهُمْ حُصُونُهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ فَأَتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَمْ يَحْتَسِبُوا۟ ۖ وَقَذَفَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلرُّعْبَ ۚ يُخْرِبُونَ بُيُوتَهُم بِأَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَيْدِى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَٱعْتَبِرُوا۟ يَـٰٓأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ
items.verseEn: It is He who expelled the ones who disbelieved among the People of the Scripture from their homes at the first gathering. You did not think they would leave, and they thought that their fortresses would protect them from Allāh; but [the decree of] Allāh came upon them from where they had not expected, and He cast terror into their hearts [so] they destroyed their houses by their [own] hands and the hands of the believers. So take warning, O people of vision.

items.verse: ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَٰتٍۢ طِبَاقًۭا ۖ مَّا تَرَىٰ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ مِن تَفَـٰوُتٍۢ ۖ فَٱرْجِعِ ٱلْبَصَرَ هَلْ تَرَىٰ مِن فُطُورٍۢ
items.verseEn: [And] who created seven heavens in layers. You do not see in the creation of the Most Merciful any inconsistency. So return [your] vision [to the sky]; do you see any breaks?

items.verse: ثُمَّ ٱرْجِعِ ٱلْبَصَرَ كَرَّتَيْنِ يَنقَلِبْ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْبَصَرُ خَاسِئًۭا وَهُوَ حَسِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: Then return [your] vision twice again. [Your] vision will return to you humbled while it is fatigued.

items.verse: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ مَا كَانَ يَنۢبَغِى لَنَآ أَن نَّتَّخِذَ مِن دُونِكَ مِنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ وَلَـٰكِن مَّتَّعْتَهُمْ وَءَابَآءَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَسُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًۢا بُورًۭا
items.verseEn: They will say, "Exalted are You! It was not for us to take besides You any allies [i.e., protectors]. But You provided comforts for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined."

harf.hint: One who bears testimony, present at the ultimate event.
harf.arabicVerse: مَّآ أَصَابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍۢ فَمِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَآ أَصَابَكَ مِن سَيِّئَةٍۢ فَمِن نَّفْسِكَ ۚ وَأَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ لِلنَّاسِ رَسُولًۭا ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًۭا
harf.verse: 4:79 — What comes to you of good is from Allāh, but what comes to you of evil, [O man], is from yourself. And We have sent you, [O Muḥammad], to the people as a messenger, and sufficient is Allāh as Witness.

deduction.title: The Scorn of a Close Relation
deduction.intro: A figure of high standing, bound by blood to a messenger, chose arrogance over faith. Their proximity to the truth only fueled their opposition, leading to a divine decree that sealed their fate and served as a timeless warning against pride and rejection. Who is this figure?
clues: I was a person of great wealth and standing among my people.
A close relative of mine began to preach a message that I vehemently opposed.
When this relative gathered our clan to deliver a warning, I was the one who publicly cursed them.
My hands were specifically cursed in the divine revelation, a direct response to my hostile actions.
My spouse was also condemned alongside me, described as one who carries thorny wood.
A whole chapter in the Quran is dedicated to my condemnation, bearing a name that refers to the blazing fire of my punishment.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Abu Jahl
Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah
Abu Lahab
Umayyah ibn Khalaf
Abdullah ibn Ubayy

trial.label: What was my primary sin?
options: Leading the army at the Battle of Badr
Building the 'Masjid al-Dirar' (Mosque of Harm)
Spreading the great slander against Aisha (ra)
Fiercely opposing and cursing the Prophet (ﷺ)
Refusing to free a believing slave
trial.answer: Fiercely opposing and cursing the Prophet (ﷺ)

location.label: Key Element
options: A beautiful garden that was destroyed
A golden calf built for worship
Thorny wood carried by my spouse
A magnificent throne of a distant kingdom
A tall tower built to reach the heavens
location.answer: Thorny wood carried by my spouse

outcome.label: Outcome
options: To be swallowed by the earth with my wealth
To be drowned in the sea with my army
To perish in a Fire of Blazing Flame
To be destroyed by a mighty, piercing cry
To have my plans exposed and die as a hypocrite
outcome.answer: To perish in a Fire of Blazing Flame

deduction.verse: May the hands of Abū Lahab be ruined, and ruined is he. (111:1)

translations: And not any moving creature in
Allah (is) its provision And He knows
its dwelling place and its place of storage All
scramble.hint: This verse affirms that the sustenance of every creature is guaranteed by the Creator.
scramble.arabic: ۞ وَمَا مِن دَآبَّةٍۢ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ رِزْقُهَا وَيَعْلَمُ مُسْتَقَرَّهَا وَمُسْتَوْدَعَهَا ۚ كُلٌّۭ فِى كِتَـٰبٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
scramble.verseEn: And there is no creature on earth but that upon Allāh is its provision, and He knows its place of dwelling and place of storage. All is in a clear register.
